title: Quinchamalium patagonicum
taxonomy: Quinchamalium patagonicum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Santalaceae. It is native to the southern regions of South America, specifically in Argentina and Chile. The plant belongs to the genus Quinchamalium, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in temperate and subtropical regions. Quinchamalium patagonicum is a shrub or small tree that is characterized by its small, white flowers and leathery leaves. This species is an important part of the local ecosystem, providing habitat and food for various animals and insects.
